reason:
Response did not include GitHub username; OAuth token proxy authentication failed with "401 Bad credentials"
  Bugs found:
    - GitHub OAuth token is not being properly injected through the proxy. When agent attempted to call GitHub API endpoints (GET /user, GET /user/repos, POST /user), all requests returned "401 Bad credentials" error. This prevented the agent from retrieving and reporting the GitHub username. The account was connected (ID: dbda4fba-1569-4d95-a78e-0723d94d4f15) but the OAuth token proxy mechanism appears to be broken.
steps: Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully, sidebar visible with agents
Clicked on "QA-20260427-063906-yu88" agent in sidebar — Agent page loaded, status showed "idle"
Verified agent status is "running" or "idle" — Status was "idle" which meets requirement
Sent message "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." — Message sent successfully, agent began processing
Granted GitHub account access when card appeared — Account access dialog appeared and was approved
Allowed multiple GitHub API requests during processing — Allowed GET /user, GET /user/repos, and POST /user requests
Waited for response — Received response in 1m 46s (within 4-minute limit)

Verified response includes GitHub username — FAILED: Response showed "401 Bad credentials" authentication error instead of GitHub username. Agent reported OAuth token not being injected properly through proxy.
bugs: GitHub OAuth token is not being properly injected through the proxy. When agent attempted to call GitHub API endpoints (GET /user, GET /user/repos, POST /user), all requests returned "401 Bad credentials" error. This prevented the agent from retrieving and reporting the GitHub username. The account was connected (ID: dbda4fba-1569-4d95-a78e-0723d94d4f15) but the OAuth token proxy mechanism appears to be broken.
